title: React - Application Performance Optimization
description: Identify and resolve unnecessary re-renders, bundle bloat, and First Contentful Paint (FCP) latency.
body:
Act as a Frontend Performance Engineer. Optimize the performance of the following React application:

<context>
Application Metrics:
- Current Bundle Size: [SIZE]
- Time to Interactive (TTI): [TIME]
- Lighthouse Score: [SCORE]
- Target Device Constraints: [Desktop/Mobile]

Observed Bottlenecks:
- Spurious re-renders in [COMPONENT_NAME].
- Unoptimized chunking/bundle size.
- High latency on First Contentful Paint.
</context>

<task>
Provide diagnostic steps and code-level solutions targeting:
1. **React Profiler**: How to pinpoint the specific rendering culprits.
2. **Memoization**: Strategic application of `React.memo`, `useMemo`, and `useCallback` without memory leaking.
3. **Code Splitting**: Implementation of `React.lazy` bundled with `Suspense` boundaries.
4. **Bundle Analysis**: Setup guide for `webpack-bundle-analyzer` or Vite equivalents.
5. **Asset Optimization**: Implementing modern image loading (e.g., `next/image`).
6. **Virtualization**: Windowing techniques for rendering massive data arrays.

Provide a targeted refactor alongside a projected before/after Lighthouse improvement analysis.
</task>
author: community

title: Vue 3 - Composition API Migration and Reusability
description: Systematically refactor legacy Vue 2 Option API components into the modern Vue 3 Composition API format.
body:
Migrate the provided Vue 2 component to the Vue 3 Composition API.

<context>
Legacy Component:
[INSERT_VUE2_OPTIONS_API_CODE]

Target Framework: Vue 3.3+
</context>

<task>
Refactor according to the following priorities:
1. Strictly enforce the `<script setup>` syntax paradigm.
2. Extract cross-component logic into standalone, reusable `composables`.
3. Implement strict type-safety utilizing TypeScript interfaces.
4. Correctly assign reactive references (`ref` vs `reactive`).
5. Migrate legacy lifecycle hooks to their modern equivalents.

Address these specific architectural patterns:
- Defining Custom Composables for shared domain logic.
- The semantic difference and application of `reactive()` vs `ref()`.
- Modern Computed Properties and deep Watchers.
- Utilizing `Teleport` for robust modal rendering.

Provide the refactored code, the newly abstracted composables, necessary TypeScript definitions, and a basic Vitest testing strategy.
</task>
author: community

title: Legacy React Class to Hooks Refactoring
description: Modernize stateful React class components to pure functional components utilizing modern Hooks.
body:
Refactor the following legacy React Class component into a functional component utilizing Hooks.

<context>
Legacy Component:
[INSERT_CLASS_COMPONENT_CODE]

State Map:
- Props: [LIST_PROPS]
- Inner State: [LIST_STATE_VARIABLES]
- Listeners/Timers: [LIST_SUBSCRIPTIONS]
- Lifecycle Overrides: [LIST_METHODS]
</context>

<task>
Execute the refactor with the following constraints:
- Retain absolute functional parity with the legacy code.
- Pre-optimize renders using `useMemo` and `useCallback` where computationally necessary.
- Abstract any complex lifecycle logic into a custom Hook.
- Inject comprehensive TypeScript definitions if the original code was pure JavaScript.

Provide the finalized refactored code block alongside a technical brief detailing the specific optimizations made.
</task>
author: jules

title: Accessibility (A11y) - WCAG 2.2 Audit and Remediation
description: Evaluate and repair web components targeting strict WCAG compliance standards.
body:
Perform a rigorous Web Accessibility (A11y) audit on the provided component markup.

<context>
Target Component/View:
[INSERT_HTML_JSX_CODE]

Application Context: [E-commerce/Dashboard/Blog]
</context>

<task>
Diagnose and resolve issues across these critical pillars:
1. **Semantic HTML**: Verify proper structural landmarks, hierarchical headings, and native element usage.
2. **ARIA Roles**: Inject missing `aria-*` state attributes and prune redundant ones.
3. **Keyboard Navigation**: Ensure absolute tab-index logic and visible focus management.
4. **Color Contrast**: Validate UI ratios against WCAG AA/AAA thresholds.
5. **Screen Readers**: Assess alternate text descriptors and content hiding mechanisms.

Provide:
- An itemized list of identified violations categorized by WCAG compliance level.
- The fully remediated code block.
- Suggested automated testing protocols (e.g., integrating `axe-core` into the CI pipeline).
</task>
author: community

title: A11y Audit - Focus Mechanics and Modal Traps
description: Engineer interactive components to be perfectly traversable via keyboard and screen readers.
body:
Audit and repair the accessibility mechanics of the following highly-interactive Modal or Dropdown component.

<context>
Target Code:
[INSERT_HTML_JSX_CODE]
</context>

<task>
Address the following mandatory accessibility checklist:
1. **Focus Trap Execution**: Ensure keyboard focus remains entirely bound within the modal overlay while active.
2. **Event Handling**: Implement robust `Esc` key interception to trigger closures.
3. **ARIA Directives**: Correctly apply `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-hidden` on background sibling nodes, and descriptive `aria-labels`.
4. **Role Assignments**: Ensure the root node possesses either `role="dialog"` or `role="menu"`.

Provide:
- The logically corrected implementation.
- A user-flow mapping of the expected keyboard navigation sequence (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Esc).
- A simulated transcript of how an industry-standard screen reader (e.g., NVDA, VoiceOver) would announce the component's entry and exit.
</task>
author: community

title: Performance Budget - Pipeline Enforcement via Lighthouse CI
description: Strictly prevent performance regressions by configuring hard budgets that block PR merges.
body:
Establish an automated Performance Budget architecture for a modern frontend repository.

<context>
Desired Hard Limits:
- Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) < 2.5s
- Total Blocking Time (TBT) < 200ms
- Compiled JS Bundle Size < 200KB (Gzipped)
</context>

<task>
Generate the following configuration suite:
1. Construct the complete `lighthouserc.json` file configuring assertions for every defined metric.
2. Write a GitHub Actions workflow script designed to run this analysis on every pull request.
3. Define the protocol for generating regression diff reports within the PR comments.

Provide the configuration files and a troubleshooting guide outlining optimization methods when the build budget inherently fails.
</task>
author: community

title: CSS - Modern Container Queries Architecture
description: Implement intrinsic web design replacing strict media queries with semantic container queries.
body:
<task>
Refactor the given legacy CSS utilizing `@media (min-width: ...)` into modern `@container` strategies.
Explain how to configure the overarching `container-type: inline-size` on the parent node and provide a polyfill strategy if supporting legacy browser variants.
</task>
author: pixelPusher_x
